value:
BASF to lay off nearly half of workforce at McIntosh facility
Chemical company plans to cut around 100 jobs at Washington County plant in 2027
MCINTOSH, Ala. (WALA) - Chemical company BASF plans to lay off around 100 employees at its McIntosh facility, nearly half of its roughly 200-person workforce.
The decision comes as part of an effort to stay competitive in what the company calls a “challenging market environment.”
The McIntosh facility currently produces specialty chemicals used in plastics and automotive products. The company said rising costs and industry pressures have made it difficult to maintain profitable operations at the site.
BASF said the plant will not close completely. Operations will continue on a smaller scale, focusing on granulation, utilities, and site services.
Production lines to shut down
In a statement, BASF said the company will cease operations of multiple production lines at its McIntosh site by the end of the first quarter of 2027, affecting approximately 100 positions.
The layoffs represent a major blow to the small community. McIntosh has a population of fewer than 500 residents.
In a statement, McIntosh Mayor Wilbert Dixon said, “The news about BASF is more than a headline—it’s a heavy weight felt in our homes, our schools, our churches, and along every street where hard-working people have built their lives. That plant has been a source of steady work for generations. It helped put food on tables, sent kids to college, and gave many of our families a sense of stability and pride.”
BASF said it is working to support impacted employees during the transition. The company said the changes will roll out over the next year, with production lines expected to shut down by early 2027.
